title: scope syntax inconsistency: 'scope s { s.spawn() }' vs 'scope |s| { }'
description: The spec uses two different syntaxes for creating a scope: ferrum-lang-concurrency.md uses 'scope s { let h = s.spawn(fetch(url)) }' (bare identifier after scope keyword), while ferrum-introduction-to-async.md uses 'scope |s| { let h = s.spawn(|...| ...) }' (closure-like |s| form). These are syntactically distinct and it is unclear if both are valid or if one is an error. A developer trying to spawn a task has two different examples and no way to know which is correct. Unify to one syntax and remove the other from the spec.
close_reason: not reproduced: both ferrum-introduction-to-async.md and ferrum-lang-concurrency.md consistently use 'scope s { s.spawn() }' — no |s| form found in current files
